answer:
**Svadharma**, or "one's own duty," is not a term that appears explicitly in the English translations of the Smriti corpus (e.g., no matches for "svadharma," "sva-dharma," "one's own dharma," or close variants). However, the concept is foundational and extensively detailed as the duties (*dharma*) proper to one's *varṇa* (Brahmaṇa, Kṣatriya, Vaiśya, or Śūdra) and *āśrama* (student, householder, forest-dweller, or renouncer). These texts emphasize adhering to one's own station over imitating another's—even if imperfectly performed—as superior for spiritual progress and social order.[^1][^2][^3]

### Varna Duties (*varṇadharma*)
Smritis prescribe hierarchical occupations, with flexibility in distress (resorting to the next lower *varṇa*'s duties, but never higher). Primary sources:

- **Viṣṇu Smṛti** (Ch. 2) concisely lists core duties:
  | Varna      | Primary Duty                  | Livelihood (in distress order)                  |
  |------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
  | Brahmaṇa  | Teaching                     | Sacrificing for others, gifts[^4]               |
  | Kṣatriya | Practice with arms           | Protecting the earth[^5]                        |
  | Vaiśya   | Tending cattle               | Agriculture, trade, etc.[^6]                    |
  | Śūdra    | Service to twice-born        | Crafts[^7]                                     |

  Common duties for all *varṇas*: forbearance, truth, etc.[^8]

- **Manusmṛti** (Ch. 10) expands with six acts for twice-born (teaching/studying/sacrificing/giving/accepting for Brahmaṇas; arms/protection for Kṣatriyas; trade/agriculture for Vaiśyas; service for Śūdras). In distress: Brahmaṇa → Kṣatriya → Vaiśya livelihoods (avoiding agriculture's violence); Śūdra → artisan work.[^9] Outsiders (*mlecchas*) and mixed castes have degraded roles (e.g., Caṇḍālas as executioners).[^10]

Similar outlines appear in Yājñavalkya Smṛti (Ch. 1, ācāra section) and others, aligning with Manu/Viṣṇu.[^11]

### Ashrama Duties (*āśramadharma*)
Primarily for twice-born (esp. Brahmaṇas); Śūdras lack full rites. **Manusmṛti** (Ch. 6) details progressive stages post-student life:

| Ashrama          | Key Duties                                                                 |
|------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Householder (*gṛhastha*) | Five sacrifices, family, wealth-support for others; highest support.[^12]  |
| Forest-dweller (*vanaprastha*) | Austerities, Vedic study, hermit foods, fires internalized.[^13]           |
| Renouncer (*saṃnyāsin*) | Wandering, alms (no Vedic study/debate), meditation, non-violence.[^14]    |

Discharge debts (to gods/teachers/ancestors) before renunciation; householder sustains all.[^15]

**Parāśara Smṛti** and others adapt for *yuga* (e.g., Kali: simplified rites), but core *varṇa*-*āśrama* framework persists.[^16]

**Caveat**: Not all 37 works (nor all Smṛtis like Kātyāyana's 80 chapters) fully elaborate; Manusmṛti/Viṣṇu are most comprehensive here. Duties interlink (e.g., all perform *saṃskāras*, avoid *adharma*). Transgressing leads to fall.[^17]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [para. p-53206d](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-53206d).
[^2]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [para. p-b34355](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-b34355).
[^3]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [para. p-4999b4](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-4999b4).
[^4]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para. p-354ee5](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-354ee5).
[^5]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para. p-f4e97b](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-f4e97b).
[^6]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para. p-6910e1](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-6910e1).
[^7]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para. p-f34593](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-f34593).
[^8]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para. p-515a60](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-515a60).
[^9]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [full chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/).
[^10]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [paras. p-ffdbb4 to p-15c243](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-ffdbb4).
[^11]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1, [key ācāra](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/).
[^12]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 6, [paras. p-5c2c8d to p-ed3d57](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-5c2c8d).
[^13]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 6, [paras. p-89d810 to p-058b16](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-89d810).
[^14]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 6, [full renouncer section](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-ed3d57).
[^15]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 6, [para. p-058b16](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-058b16).
[^16]: Parāśara Smṛti, [work overview](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/_work/wiki/).
[^17]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 10, [para. p-233a88](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-233a88).
